page.content:
The data presented in the accompanying table offers a comprehensive overview of the regional variations in the prevalence of KSR1 over a seven-year period, from 2008 to 2014. This analysis is crucial in understanding the trends and shifts in KSR1 prevalence across different regions, which can inform future research and health policy decisions. Notably, the columns for each year reflect the percentage of the population exhibiting KSR1, revealing both increases and fluctuations that merit further investigation. 

The inclusion of the "HR (95% CI)" column provides additional context, offering insights into the hazard ratios and their corresponding confidence intervals, which are essential for evaluating the statistical significance of the observed trends.

page.content: The accompanying "P-value" metric serves to ascertain the strength of the associations, indicating the likelihood that the observed patterns are not due to random chance. By analyzing these metrics, stakeholders can develop targeted interventions aimed at addressing disparities in KSR1 prevalence across the specified regions, thereby enhancing public health outcomes and optimizing resource allocation. Overall, this data serves as a vital tool in guiding future research and policy initiatives related to KSR1.
